summary.intent: Triage and execute on four Tier-3 open items (CI workflow, --run-dir autosave, score history, spec-file loader), then dogfood the optimize-anything tool on real artifacts (CLAUDE.md, README.md) to validate the LLM judge path and surface product gaps.
summary.outcome: Discovered all Tier-3 items were already code-complete; dogfooded on CLAUDE.md and README.md which exposed three LLM judge bottlenecks; applied structural README improvements (-118 lines, all tests passing); designed the `analyze` subcommand as the correct framing for plateau-breaking; committed two clean commits with design docs and handoff updated.

repo: All four Tier-3 items (CI workflow, --run-dir autosave, score history, spec-file loader) were code-complete in commit 9dc9448 — project memory tracking was stale
222 tests collected across the test suite; 214 pass excluding integration tests
skill_clarity.sh header comment listed weights as 0.30/0.30/0.25/0.15 but the actual WEIGHTS dict was 0.25/0.25/0.20/0.30 — comment was canonical discrepancy, code was correct
docs/ directory is gitignored — all design docs and handoffs are local-only reference files
gepa's default reflection_lm proposer may be unavailable in practice; --model must be passed explicitly on every invocation
LLM judge (Claude Sonnet) showed remarkable stability — 0.88 across 9+ independent evaluations of CLAUDE.md with consistent reasoning
Quality dimensions vs vague objective creates measurable scoring headroom: same README scores 0.88 (vague) vs 0.9175 (specific dimensions)
auto-refine is only relevant for --judge-model + vague objective on near-ceiling artifacts; heuristic evaluators, HTTP evaluators, and well-specified dimensions never need it
LLM judges penalize structural reorganization even when it improves document UX — a known judge conservatism bias

code.finding: Header comment had wrong weight values; fixed to match actual WEIGHTS dict (structure=0.25, actionability=0.25, specificity=0.20, conciseness=0.30)

Doc contract tests assert README contains specific intake schema terms: artifact_class, hard_constraints, evaluator_cwd, best_artifact, total_metric_calls, score_summary, top_diagnostics, plateau_guidance, evaluator_failure_signal — these must be preserved in any README restructure

workflow: Dogfooding on already-polished artifacts (CLAUDE.md, README.md) proves tool correctly refuses degradation but doesn't surface visible improvement — mediocre seed + clear scoring criteria is the right demo use case
gpt-4o-mini as proposer makes sweeping rewrites that lose as much as they gain; claude-sonnet makes structurally bolder edits but judge still penalized them
When gepa plateaus, the bottleneck is often evaluation criteria precision, not proposer capability — specific quality dimensions are the most effective lever
Cherry-picking structural improvements from failed LLM mutations is a valid manual workflow even when automated optimization fails
Subagent-driven development pattern: handle trivial tasks directly, dispatch subagents only for tasks requiring interactive review or API calls
The right framing for plateau-breaking is an `analyze` pre-optimization subcommand, not a magic runtime loop — positions it as one tool in the toolkit, not the core product

friction: Tier-3 open items were fully code-complete but tracked as open in project memory — caused unnecessary investigation and re-scoping at session start
Both CLAUDE.md and README.md plateau at 0.88; gepa exhausted 7 mutations in budget=15 runs without any improvement, suggesting the tool's default stopping heuristic is opaque
Doc contract tests in test_doc_contract.py broke silently after README restructure — intake schema terms were removed; required manual diagnosis and targeted fix

LLM judge conservatism penalized Sonnet's genuinely better README restructure (quickstart-first, concise examples) — the tool scored the original higher despite the mutation being objectively better for users
HANDOFF.md was not updated to point to the latest session's handoff doc until the user explicitly caught it at end of session
open_items: Implement `analyze` subcommand: new CLI command + analyze_for_dimensions() in llm_judge.py — see docs/plans/2026-02-25-analyze-subcommand-design.md
Add plateau detection advisory: post-run suggestions when 0 improvements found across all iterations
Architecture diagram — never built despite being referenced across multiple sessions
CI verification: confirm GitHub Actions green run with secrets configured; 2 commits ahead of origin, not yet pushed
Public release prep (item 4 from user's priority list)
